feedback.text: I would like to request that protections of some form be added to already sold video games to help prevent them from being destroyed, removed or otherwise unfairly taken from the people who have bought them. The citizen's initiative Stop Killing Games has over 1 million signatures which shows that there is widespread support for the requirement for publishers and developers to enact some form of "end of life" service, such as releasing server code, or similar. As such I think that the Digital Fairness Act should also protect video games. Thank you for your consideration!
